Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a classification for news articles, allowing users to browse content by topic.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The human-readable name of the category (e.g., 'Politics', 'Sports').

description.description: A brief description of the category's scope.

slug.description: A URL-friendly, unique identifier for the category, derived from its name, suitable for use in URLs.

Author.title: Author
Author.description: Represents an individual who writes and publishes news articles.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Author entity.

name.description: The full name of the author.

bio.description: A short biographical description of the author.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the author's profile picture.

Image.title: Image
Image.description: Represents an image asset that can be associated with articles or other content.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Image entity.

url.description: The URL or path to the image file.

altText.description: Alternative text for the image, crucial for accessibility and SEO.

caption.description: A short caption or description displayed with the image.

uploadedDate.description: The date and time when the image was uploaded.

photographerName.description: The name of the photographer or source of the image, if applicable.

Tag.title: Tag
Tag.description: Represents a keyword or label used to categorize articles for easier search and related content discovery.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Tag entity.

name.description: The human-readable name of the tag (e.g., 'Election', 'Innovation').

slug.description: A URL-friendly, unique identifier for the tag, derived from its name, suitable for use in URLs.

Article.title: Article
Article.description: Represents a single news article, containing its content, metadata, and references to related entities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Article entity.

title.description: The headline or title of the news article.

content.description: The full body text of the article, typically in Markdown or HTML format.

publicationDate.description: The date and time when the article was originally published.

lastModifiedDate.description: The date and time when the article was last updated or modified.

status.description: The current publication status of the article (e.g., 'draft', 'published', 'archived').

summary.description: A concise, AI-generated summary of the article's content, providing a quick overview.

slug.description: A URL-friendly, unique identifier for the article, derived from its title, suitable for use in URLs.

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category the article belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N Article)

authorId.description: Reference to the Author who wrote the article. (Relationship: Author 1:N Article)

featuredImageId.description: Reference to the primary Image used as the featured visual for the article. (Relationship: Article 1:1 Image)

tagIds.description: References to Tags associated with the article for enhanced search and categorization. (Relationship: Tag N:N Article)

definition.description: Collection for administrative roles. Each document ID (`adminId`) corresponds to a user's UID, where the existence of a document grants administrative privileges. The 'Author' schema is employed as a general representation for a person's identity in this context.
Publicly readable collection of news categories. Admins have write access.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique ID of the category.

definition.description: Publicly readable collection of news authors. Admins have write access.

params.name: authorId
params.description: The unique ID of the author.

definition.description: Publicly readable collection of image assets used in articles. Admins have write access.

params.name: imageId
params.description: The unique ID of the image.

definition.description: Publicly readable collection of tags for news articles. Admins have write access.

params.name: tagId
params.description: The unique ID of the tag.

definition.description: Collection for published news articles, which are publicly readable. Admins have write access for updates/deletions. Documents in this collection always represent a 'published' state.

params.name: articleId
params.description: The unique ID of the published article.

definition.description: Collection for news articles in 'draft' status, accessible only by administrators. Documents in this collection always represent a 'draft' state.

params.name: articleId
params.description: The unique ID of the draft article.

definition.description: Collection for news articles in 'archived' status, accessible only by administrators. Documents in this collection always represent an 'archived' state.

params.name: articleId
params.description: The unique ID of the archived article.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for InfoKilat prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable access patterns by adhering to core design principles, especially Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation.

**Authorization Independence:**
This design completely avoids hierarchical authorization dependencies (`get()` calls in security rules). For instance, an article's access permissions do not depend on reading its associated category or author document. All necessary authorization context is either self-contained within the document (e.g., an article's status implicitly determines its collection, and thus its public/admin accessibility) or handled via a dedicated, independent roles collection.

Admin roles are implemented using the DBAC (Database Access Control) pattern. A dedicated collection, `/roles_admin/{adminId}`, contains marker documents where the `adminId` is the user's UID. The mere existence of a document in this collection grants administrative privileges, allowing rules to check `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` without needing to fetch other user data.

**QAPs (Queries as Authorization Primitives):**
Secure `list` operations are a cornerstone of this design, achieved primarily through **Structural Segregation**. Instead of mixing documents with different security postures in one collection and relying on rules to filter, content is separated into distinct collections based on its access requirements:
*   `/published_articles`: All documents in this collection are publicly readable, making `list` operations trivially secure for public users (`allow read: true`).
*   `/admin_drafts` and `/archived_articles`: These collections are exclusively for administrators. `list` operations for these collections are securely restricted to authenticated administrators (`allow read: if isAdmin()`).

This segregation ensures that security rules are simple, explicit, and performant. Public users querying `/published_articles` do not need to be aware of or filtered from `draft` or `archived` content, as that content simply does not exist in their accessible path. Similarly, reference data like `/categories`, `/authors`, `/images`, and `/tags` are universally public, simplifying their access rules to `allow read: true`.

**Overall Structure:**
*   **Public Read, Admin Write Collections:** `/categories`, `/authors`, `/images`, `/tags`, `/published_articles`. These collections hold data that is globally visible for read operations but requires administrative privileges for creation, updates, or deletions.
*   **Admin-Only Collections:** `/roles_admin`, `/admin_drafts`, `/archived_articles`. These collections are exclusively for administrative functions, ensuring strict control over sensitive content and role management.
*   **Explicit State Modeling:** Article status is managed structurally (by moving articles between `/admin_drafts`, `/published_articles`, `/archived_articles`) rather than solely relying on a `status` field within a single collection, reinforcing security and clarity.

This architecture provides a clear, maintainable, and highly secure foundation for the InfoKilat news platform.
